subject: english
learning_objectives: State the rules for doubling a final consonant before adding a suffix
Apply the rules correctly to one-syllable and multi-syllable words
Handle special cases: words ending in -p and -c

sections.heading: Rule 1 ‚Äì One-syllable words (one vowel + one consonant)
sections.content:
Double the final consonant before a suffix beginning with a vowel.
big‚Üíbigger | bat‚Üíbatting | drop‚Üídropped | drum‚Üídrummer

Exception ‚Äì do NOT double when the vowel is already doubled (digraph):
boat‚Üíboating | boil‚Üíboiled | cheap‚Üícheapest | sweet‚Üísweeter
Special exception: wool‚Üíwoollen

sections.heading: Rule 2 ‚Äì Multi-syllable words: accent on last syllable
sections.content:
Double the final consonant when the stress falls on the last syllable.
ad-MIT‚Üíadmittance | be-GIN‚Üíbeginning | for-GET‚Üíforgetting | oc-CUR‚Üíoccurrence

sections.heading: Rule 3 ‚Äì Multi-syllable words: accent NOT on last syllable
sections.content:
Do NOT double the final consonant.
EN-ter‚Üíentering | HAP-pen‚Üíhappened | IN-habit‚Üíinhabitant | RE-fer‚Üíreference

sections.heading: Rule 4 ‚Äì Words ending in -p
sections.content:
A final 'p' is doubled:
handicap‚Üíhandicapped | kidnap‚Üíkidnapped | worship‚Üíworshipped
Exception: develop‚Üídeveloped (NOT developped)

sections.heading: Rule 5 ‚Äì Words ending in -c
sections.content:
Add 'k' before the suffix to preserve the hard /k/ sound:
frolic‚Üífrolicked | mimic‚Üímimicked | panic‚Üípanicky
